value_picks.name: Boston Scientific Corporation

fcf_yield.detail: FCF $2.807B / mcap $71.32B = 3.94%, just under the 4% threshold.

pe_discount.detail: Forward PE 12.82 vs implied PE 20.84 (target $78 / fwd EPS $3.74) = 38.5% discount.

drawdown.detail: Down 56.18% from 52w high of $109.50; trading $0.63 above 52w low.

balance_sheet.detail: Net debt ($1.45B cash vs $11.03B debt) but D/E 42.25 (<50) and current ratio 1.9 (>1.2) clear the secondary test.

dividend.detail: No dividend (payout_ratio 0.0); treated as neutral per framework.

institutional.detail: Institutions hold 94.9%; rec_mean 1.48 with 27 of 31 analysts at buy/strong-buy and zero sells.

ai_exposure.detail: Medical device pipeline (electrophysiology mapping, FARAPULSE PFA, endoscopy SpyGlass) increasingly leverages AI image analysis and procedural guidance; AI accelerates device R&D rather than disrupting recurring procedure revenue.

deal_radar.assessment: Short interest rose 22.7% MoM to 2.12% of float with only 1.65 days-to-cover; mild bearish positioning into a -56% drawdown that already prices in significant deceleration.

catalysts_general: Trading 38.5% below analyst median target of $78 with 30 analysts and rec_mean 1.48
PEG 0.59 with revenue growth 11.6% and forward EPS $3.74 vs trailing $2.39 (+56% expected)
Operating margin 20.6% and gross margin 68.9% intact through the drawdown
Price sits within 1.3% of 52-week low — capitulation zone

downside_scenarios.scenario: Break of 52w low; multiple compresses on med-device sector derate or tariff/reimbursement headline.
Forward EPS revised down 10% to $3.37 (current-year est) and market assigns trough multiple.
Earnings miss + guidance cut; gross-margin compression pulls re-rating toward legacy med-device trough multiples.

ideal_entry.reasoning: Stage scale-in just under 52w low ($47.35) to capture maximum capitulation; risk-reward at 38% discount to analyst target is favorable with operating margin still 20%+.

comparables.note: Medtronic — slower growth, lower multiple; BSX deserves a premium for 11.6% revenue growth and PFA momentum (not in input dataset).
Abbott — broader portfolio commands higher multiple; BSX trades at a relative discount despite stronger growth (not in input dataset).

value_picks.analyst_take: BSX has been thrown out with the broader healthcare/med-device basket despite double-digit revenue growth, mid-teens ROE, and PFA momentum. 38.5% discount to analyst consensus with 30 analysts at rec_mean 1.48 and the stock pinned to its 52w low offers asymmetric upside; FCF yield falling just shy of 4% is the only meaningful blemish.

fcf_yield.detail: FCF $5.232B / mcap $96.77B = 5.41%, in the 4-7% borderline band.

pe_discount.detail: Forward PE 12.95 vs implied PE 17.66 (target $482.50 / fwd EPS $27.32) = 26.7% discount, just shy of pass.

dividend.detail: Yield 1.36% (<2%) with low payout 28.3%; meets borderline criteria (yield>0 AND payout<90).

institutional.detail: Institutions 92.2%; rec_mean 1.53 with 28 of 34 analysts buy/strong-buy and zero sells.

ai_exposure.detail: Generative AI is both threat (free AI-powered tax filing chips at TurboTax moat; Credit Karma decisioning could be commoditized) and tailwind (Intuit Assist embedded across QuickBooks/TurboTax expands ARPU). Net: balanced — execution-dependent.

deal_radar.assessment: Short interest up 17% MoM to 4.62% of float with 4.18 days-to-cover — modest skepticism, not crowded short, ahead of fiscal Q4.

catalysts_general: Trading 26.7% below median analyst target of $482.50 (32 analysts, rec_mean 1.53)
Forward PE 12.95 is the lowest in years for a 47% operating margin, 22% ROE software business
Revenue growth 10.4% and earnings growth 10.7% with 80.8% gross margins intact
PEG 0.86 with conservative payout 28% leaves room for dividend hikes/buybacks
Stock 53% off ATH despite no fundamental deterioration in the most recent reported quarter

downside_scenarios.scenario: Test of 52w low on weak tax season metrics or Credit Karma soft guide.
Break of 52w low; AI disruption narrative gains traction and software sector derates further.
Forward EPS revised down 15% and trough multiple as small-business segment cyclical weakness shows.

ideal_entry.reasoning: Stage in slightly above 52w low ($300.50) — sub-12x forward PE for a 47% op-margin franchise rarely persists; leaves room to add lower if break-down occurs.

comparables.note: Per input — ADSK faster top-line, higher multiple; INTU offers larger absolute margin profile and dividend.
Mega-cap software peers trade at 2x INTU's multiple for similar/lower growth (not in input dataset).

value_picks.analyst_take: INTU at 12.95x forward earnings is a generational reset for a 47% operating margin, 22% ROE franchise growing top-line 10%+. The AI-disruption fear is real but overstated — Intuit Assist is more likely to expand ARPU than to be displaced. Buy aggressively into the $300s; risk-reward to analyst median $482.50 is ~36% upside vs ~12% downside to recent low.
